category: multi-variant page where the default variant is a small sampler

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text or embedded variant data. 'Seattle, WA' appears in the footer, supporting roaster location, and 'USD' is supported by embedded variant prices. 'is_espresso=false' is supported because the page says it is roasted for filter brewing, though adventurous espresso drinkers may enjoy it, which does not make it an espresso product. 'is_specialty_coffee=true' is supported by the microlot/producer/process-focused specialty coffee product presentation.

realism.reasons: Very plausible as a real specialty coffee product page. The structure matches common Shopify-style html-to-text extraction: header nav, title/vendor, price, size selector, quantity/add-to-cart, descriptive sections, related products, embedded variant metadata, and footer. The coffee details are coherent and appropriately specific for specialty roasting: producer (Nestor Lasso / El Diviso), region hierarchy (Huila, Bruselas, Pitalito), variety (Sidra), process (Natural), altitude, harvest window, and sensory notes all fit known industry patterns. The copy voice is credible—concise, lightly marketing-driven, and coffee-literate without sounding exaggerated. The process description and explicit 'No co-ferments, added fruit, or flavorings' note also reflects current real-world market language around Colombian experimental lots. Pricing is believable for a premium Sidra natural, especially with a 100g sampler option. Variant block looks realistic and internally consistent: IDs, titles, full names, SKU formatting, package weights, and price scaling all make sense. Only very minor tells: the exact combination of harvest dates, footer year, and polished completeness could be synthetic, but nothing stands out as implausible.

label.coffee.sensory_text:
Tasting notes: Strawberry jam, pink lemonade, cacao nib
This lot from El Diviso is all about saturated fruit and a sparkling finish. We taste ripe strawberry jam up front, a bright pink lemonade acidity through the middle, and a soft cacao nib note as it cools.
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: Strawberry jam, pink lemonade, cacao nib
label.coffee.producer_text: Nestor Lasso and the team at El Diviso have become known for carefully separated microlots grown around Bruselas in Huila. For this Sidra selection, ripe cherries were floated, hand sorted, and dried as a natural process lot on raised beds with close attention to temperature and airflow. No co-ferments, added fruit, or flavorings are used in this coffee.
